text: Came in after a Saturday round — the green chile breakfast burrito hit the spot and the coffee kept coming.
Quiet on a weekday morning, which I appreciate. Service was attentive without hovering.
Solid patio for the cooler months. Salmon was cooked right and the iced tea was actually fresh-brewed.
Tucked off Pima Road, easy parking. The southwest salad is generous and the staff knew the menu cold.
Reliable spot for a post-museum lunch. Nothing fancy, but everything came out hot and on time.
Better than the chains nearby. The grilled chicken was tender and the prices are fair for Scottsdale.
Took Judith here for an anniversary dinner — the kitchen handled a low-sodium request without fuss.
Busy at peak but the line moves. Worth the short wait for the carne asada plate.
Friendly owner who remembered us the second visit. Good local feel, not a tourist trap.
Clean, comfortable, and the booths give you room to spread out a newspaper. My kind of morning.
The fish tacos were fresh and the salsa had real heat. Will be back next time we're in the area.
Decent wine list for a casual place. The Paso Robles red paired well with the steak.
Good value lunch special. The soup of the day was homemade and the bread basket was warm.
A little pricey, but the patio view of the McDowells makes up for it at sunset.
Quick, kind service on a hot afternoon. The cold brew and the turkey club did the job.
Brought the grandkids — kids' menu was real food, not just nuggets. Parents appreciated that.
Consistent every visit. The omelet is dependable and the hash browns are crisp, not greasy.
Great after a hike near Sedona on the drive home. Hearty portions and honest prices.
Staff went out of their way when we came in just before closing. No attitude, real hospitality.
The patio heaters make winter dining comfortable. Espresso was strong and well pulled.
Neighborhood favorite for good reason — fresh ingredients, fair prices, and they get the order right.
Came in after a Saturday round - the green chile breakfast burrito hit the spot and the coffee kept coming.
Solid clubhouse lunch. Grilled salmon done right and a patio with a mountain view. Tom approved.
Reliable pro shop. Regripped my irons same day and didn't upsell me on anything.
Tested this steakhouse before recommending it to Ed - quiet, good service, the ribeye was excellent.
Great low-key spot for Judith and me on a weeknight. Southwest chicken salad is a regular order now.
Coffee before a dawn tee time. Fast, hot, and they open early enough for golfers.
Best green chile in north Scottsdale, hands down. We drive across town for it.
Good bookstore with a real aviation and military-history section. Found two Connelly first editions.
Quick oil change on the 4Runner before a park trip. In and out, fair price.
Nice patio for jazz night. Wine list is reasonable and the kitchen doesn't rush you.
Cardiology office runs on time and the staff is kind. Rare combination.
Museum cafe surprised me - decent sandwiches and the docents get a discount.
